methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Fuse Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by JC Btaiche, currently valued at $200M+ post-money following $78M in total fundraising. The nuclear energy sector comp universe includes 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies, with notable private comparables including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, General Matter, and Standard Nuclear.

The valuation analysis applies a single methodologically sound approach: stage-progression analysis. This method takes the company's $200M last post-money valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $400M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient comparable data points; while 3 same-sector peers exist, none met the filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) required to generate a reliable cohort of three or more datapoints.

The triangulated valuation range is $340M to $460M, with a median point of $400M. This single-method result reflects meaningful uncertainty: the narrow evidentiary base limits confidence in the range, and the lack of direct peer comparables means the analysis cannot validate the stage-progression assumption against observed market behavior in this sub-sector. Key risks include execution on commercialization timelines, regulatory pathway clarity, and whether capital requirements escalate beyond peer patterns.
